name: ESSAI 2026
venue: European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence, Vienna, Austria
description: The 4th European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence (ESSAI) will be held in Vienna, Austria from 6 to 10 July 2026. It offers introductory and advanced AI courses, specialized tutorials, and tracks on Bilateral AI and Digital Humanism, targeting PhD students, young researchers, and senior scholars. The program also includes networking opportunities, a rich social agenda, and a reception at Vienna City Hall.

name: OpenEuroLLM Winter School
description: The 2026 Winter School on Multilinguality in LLM Development and Evaluation is held at the Skeikampen Conference Hotel north of Oslo, Norway. It targets NLP researchers, especially those from EuroHPC countries and doctoral candidates, and aims to strengthen collaboration and share expertise on large‑scale multilingual language models using high‑performance e‑infrastructures. The program features technical talks, hands‑on sessions, and discussions on open‑science practices, multilingual data coverage, and evaluation methods, all provided free of charge apart from travel and accommodation costs.

name: Probabilistic AI School
description: The Probabilistic AI School 2026 will take place in Vilnius, Lithuania from August 3‑7, 2026. It aims to provide an inclusive educational environment covering state‑of‑the‑art machine‑learning and artificial‑intelligence topics. Enthusiasts, students, academics, and industry professionals are invited to participate and network.

name: BMVA Computer Vision Summer School
description: The 29th BMVA Computer Vision Summer School will be held at Durham University in Durham, England, UK. It offers an intensive week of lectures and lab sessions covering a broad spectrum of computer‑vision topics, delivered by leading researchers from the UK and abroad. The programme features daily oral talks and hands‑on sessions designed for graduate students and early‑career researchers interested in the latest advances in the field.

name: Oxford Machine Learning Summer School 2026
description: OxML Summer 2026 is a machine‑learning school organized by the Oxford ML School, offering both in‑person and online participation. The programme provides three specialised tracks covering the latest AI theory and applications, taught by world‑renowned experts from academia and industry. Prospective participants can combine tracks to match their learning goals, and registration details will be announced soon.

name: Robotics, Perception and Learning (RPL) Summer School
venue: KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Stockholm, Sweden
description: The RPL Summer School 2026 is a six‑day event for doctoral and postdoctoral researchers organized by the Department for Robotics, Perception and Learning at KTH Royal Institute of Technology in Stockholm, Sweden. It covers a broad spectrum of topics such as machine learning, computer vision, robotics, human‑robot interaction, planning and decision making, and combines keynote talks, industry panels, student presentations, and open brainstorming sessions. Held at the Happy Tammsvik resort, the school aims to foster new collaborations by providing a relaxed setting with indoor and outdoor activities, fully funded accommodation, meals, and materials for participants.

name: Machine Learning Crash Course at MALGA
description: The MLCC2026 Summer School – Machine Learning Crash Course – provides an introduction to fundamental machine‑learning concepts and algorithms. It is aimed at undergraduate and graduate students, as well as professionals, and is open to participants from any university after a selection process. The five‑day in‑person program in Genoa combines theoretical lectures with hands‑on lab sessions and includes a final test consisting of notebook work and a report.

name: UK Robotics Summer School
venue: National Robotarium, Heriot-Watt University, Edinburgh, United Kingdom
description: The UK Robotics Summer School 2026 is a five‑day program held at the National Robotarium, Heriot‑Watt University in Edinburgh, United Kingdom. It offers expert‑led lectures, tutorials and industry talks covering bio‑inspired robotics, healthcare robotics, generative AI for robotics, safe AI and real‑world applications, with hands‑on experience using state‑of‑the‑art technology. The school targets master’s‑level students and industry practitioners, providing networking opportunities and a special dinner.

name: International Summer School for Advanced Studies on Biometrics, Behavior, and Vision
description: The 2026 Biometrics Summer School takes place in Alghero, Sardinia, Italy, hosted at the Hotel Dei Pini. It focuses on the impact of generative AI and large foundation models on biometric and human‑recognition technologies, covering machine learning, image understanding, signal analysis, neuroscience, robotics, forensic and digital forensics. The program is aimed at PhD students, post‑docs, researchers and professionals, offering intensive lectures by 20 international experts, hands‑on sessions, and the possibility of scholarships and a short‑paper presentation.

name: 1st Internationa School on the Cognitive, Ethical, and Societal Dimensions of AI - AIces
description: AIces 2026 is the first International School on the Cognitive, Ethical and Societal Dimensions of Artificial Intelligence, bringing together multidisciplinary discussions on AI foundations, challenges and risks. The programme is aimed at graduate and postgraduate students as well as industry practitioners, offering parallel courses, a symposium for short presentations, and open‑debate sessions across the three thematic areas. Participants can attend face‑to‑face in Porto–Maia, Portugal, or join live online, with all lectures recorded and available for 45 days after the event, and receive a certificate of participation.

name: Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal (ELS) aspects of AI and AS at Umeå University
description: The Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal aspects of AI and Autonomous Systems brings together researchers and students to explore the interdisciplinary challenges of emerging technologies. It offers a program of lectures, hands‑on multidisciplinary training, a poster session, and a social program to foster networking and professional exchange. The event takes place at Umeå University in Sweden from 11 to 13 March 2026.

name: Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM 2026)
description: The Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM’27) will be held at the Escandille centre in Autrans, in the Vercors mountains near Grenoble, France. It targets PhD students and early‑career researchers, offering lectures, hands‑on sessions and informal networking on topics such as computer vision, speech processing, affective computing and human‑robot interaction. The mountain setting also provides opportunities for outdoor activities, fostering a relaxed scientific retreat and encouraging new collaborations across academia and industry.

name: Northern Lights Deep Learning Winter School
venue: UiT The Arctic University of Norway, Tromsø, Norway
description: The 2027 NLDL Winter School is part of the Northern Lights Deep Learning Conference and will be held in Tromsø, Norway. It runs from 11 to 15 January 2027 and focuses on recent advances in deep learning, including image classification, object detection, and speech recognition. The school targets researchers and practitioners seeking to exchange ideas and foster collaborations in the field.

name: Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS)
description: The Sixth Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS) is an 11‑day intensive program offering AI lectures, hands‑on labs, and project work. It targets a diverse audience including senior undergraduates, graduate and PhD students, faculty members, and industry professionals from around the world. The school brings together over 500 in‑person participants and thousands of livestream viewers to explore fundamentals and cutting‑edge applications of artificial intelligence.

name: EEML Summer School
venue: Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School, Cetinje, Montenegro
description: The Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School takes place in Cetinje, Montenegro from 27 July to 1 August 2026. The program offers a series of lectures and tutorials on machine‑learning research presented by experts from institutions such as Google DeepMind, MIT, University of Oxford and others. It is aimed at students and early‑career researchers interested in advanced topics in AI and provides networking opportunities with leading academics and industry professionals.

name: Mediterranean Summer School
description: The Mediterranean Machine Learning (M2L) Summer School is a five‑day program that combines keynotes, lectures and hands‑on sessions led by international AI experts. It targets doctoral and outstanding master students, as well as academics and practitioners, especially from the Mediterranean region, and covers topics such as computer vision, natural language processing, generative models, reinforcement learning and ethics in machine learning. Participants can present posters, receive feedback, and network through social activities and informal interactions with speakers and sponsors.

name: Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI
venue: Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications, Rabat, Morocco
description: The 2nd edition of the AI Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI is a five‑day immersive program jointly organized by MoroccoAI, INPT, IMT Nord Europe, and Soft Centre. It targets master, Ph.D., post‑doc, and early‑career researchers as well as industry professionals, offering theoretical lectures, hands‑on labs, and project mentorship on cutting‑edge AI topics. The school combines expert instruction, networking events, and practical workshops to build the next generation of AI leaders in Morocco.

name: AthNLP Summer School 2026
description: The Athens Natural Language Processing Summer School 2026 (AthNLP) will be held at the NCSR Demokritos campus in Athens, Greece. The program offers intensive lectures and hands‑on sessions on state‑of‑the‑art NLP techniques for graduate students and researchers. It is organized jointly by several institutions, including RC Athena, NCSR Demokritos, Athens University of Economics and Business, Heriot‑Watt University, and the ELLIS‑University of Manchester partnership.

name: Advanced Artificial Intelligence: GenAI & Deep Unsupervised Learning
venue: Lady Margaret Hall, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: The Advanced Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning summer school at Lady Margaret Hall focuses on generative AI and deep unsupervised learning techniques. It provides theoretical foundations and hands‑on experience with clustering, GANs, self‑supervised and semi‑supervised methods, emphasizing real‑world applications across domains. The programme is aimed at STEM students with intermediate AI/ML knowledge who are considering graduate study or research, and it is offered both residentially and online.

name: AI4Science Summer School
description: The 2nd Summer School on Advancing Scientific Discovery with AI brings together researchers, PhD students and post‑doctoral scholars in Caen, Normandy, France. It focuses on applying deep‑learning techniques to accelerate scientific research, covering topics such as machine learning for data analysis, AI‑driven experimental design, generative models, and integration of AI with physical simulations. The program combines lectures with hands‑on Python workshops using libraries like NumPy and PyTorch to give participants practical experience in implementing AI models for scientific problems.

name: LUT Summer School
description: LUT Summer School is a one‑week intensive program offering bachelor’s and master’s level courses in sustainability, climate action, marketing, management and the intersection of business and technology. The courses take place on the Lappeenranta campus of LUT University from 3 – 7 August 2026 and combine classroom teaching, workshops, group work and social activities in the Finnish summer environment. The programme is aimed at international students enrolled in higher‑education institutions and includes optional online components, a short‑exchange credit structure and a vibrant social programme.
